reviews.review_text:
This is a roguelike game where skills mainly matters. Sure, there's RNG elements but there's controlled elements as well that help you "shape" your runs. For better or worse.

And I personally would always choose skill over arbitrary RNG in roguelikes. It's enjoyable, fluid and very challenging. Highly recommended for those seeking to conquer such games.

Simply amazing. The gameplay loop is fresh, with infinite replayability. The graphics are beautiful and I'll never get tired of looking at the sprites and backrounds. And the soundtrack is a bop. 10/10

Top tier gaming and perfect to relax. Once you get into the flow you become an absolute unit. Love the references, the lore and the funky sound track. Graphics hooked me and the action made me stay.

You will die. But that is just the beginning.

I have been sick of all the roguelites that lack feasible build variety. No matter what they offer, only one path really works. So I came back to this, and this is how you do it. I actually feel like I'm not playing the exact same game loop over and over, only searching for specific mechanics while shedding everything else. A different combination of weapons and upgrades can work as well as another! Rare in this day in age when everything is either "souls like" or a roguelite, that the game actually meets the basic standards of those genre tags.

this game didn't hook me but never felt like a waste of time/money. Great design. Fun to pick up and play for 10-20 minutes per session. I felt like I knew the art style after a short preview, but kept finding myself pleasantly surprised by little animations included for new actions. writing this review after accumulating 20 hours over about a year of on-and-off playing.
I may not be great at the game but wow is it still a lot of fun to play. Its oddly satisfying making your way through everything..

great game 
 
it have many weapons and the maps every time it change 

there are maps in dlc if you want full exprince  if you die you well being from start  you will drop game many time 
 i love the game ,do i recommend if you have money i will ,  if you have not that money i dont 
6.5-7-8/10 (you will hate your self )
This is one of the best games out there. The combat is stimulating and very fast. Not to mention the story is deep and fun to uncover. 10/10
bought it yesterday and immediately bought like every dlc very worth

dead cells to me was my first proper dive into the metroidvania genre and it was an amazing one! it was a learning curve to overcome and im glad i did, from the weapons to the movement to the animations during gameplay, i loved every second of it and further opened my eyes toward new games of similar genres i have in my library. TLDR: first proper metropidvania experiance, amazing game overall and am a fan of the genre.

After beating 0bc to 2bc on my best account and 1bc one my worst thorughout my four different acounts (steam is by far my least played account) i can say dead cells is one of the best games i have, it is 100% worth full price if you like fast 2d action games, but waiting for a sale is never a bad idea.

Runs are shorter then games like risk of rain 2 (about a hour) dead cells runs are about 30-40 mintues, but with good auto saving so you can stop half way through and keep going later.

Works great on Steam Deck.

You will die a lot, but it does not awalys feel like losing, unless you go to slumbering sanctuery and have to fight one of those golems, never go there if you don't have to.

Every run feels different, but not like risk of rain where the maps are just in a random order, the map changes every time.

Movement is the best 2d game movement i have seen. Very smooth and reactive.

Overall i rate Dead Cells a 9/10.
one of the most difficult games that i played! first finished it on mobile and now on pc! great game GG
Makes me want to tear out my organs then swallow them while amputating my own limbs. Love this game, 10/10

Great game, however, pretty hard. If you are looking to sit in your sofa, grab your Steam Deck or controller to play and relax, this game will NOT be it. Overall though, fun game, meant to be played over and over and over again, so if you beat it in an hour or 2, don't be surprised that's the end of the game. Play it over.
Give it time and it gets more fun, tough but fair. Definitely change your keybindings from the default lol
addictive game flow that grows to require nothing but absolute precision
Cool gameplay & graphics, fun to play but STOP FLYING TOWARDS ME THROUGHT WALLS

I probably have 100+ hours on this on the Nintendo Switch and a couple more here because this game is easily one of the best pick-up-and-play rougelites I've played. Not too easy, not too difficult, with a ton of weapons, costumes, and skills to unlock. It's an awesome dive for when you want to play a game when you want to take a break from lengthy RPGs.
A really fun game to play. The only problem for me is that it is very time consuming.

Though I'm fairly new to this game with only 30 hours on record, I recommend this game completely. The pixel art, mechanics, replayability, everything is just top tier. Though some enemies and/or biomes may be challenging and aggravating, with enough patience and determination, anyone can overcome these hurdles.
Great game! My only problem is that some content is locked behind 4 paid dlcs. Still, its a must buy!

This game runs at 60 fps on the worst computer ever ((mine) literally the worst computer ever) and still has me discovering content I hadn't previously seen either through difficulty or unlocking. Anyways, its pretty good. 10/10.
A challenging and addictive side scroller.
only just scratched the surface. endless possibilities. + killing monsters is fun

I played seven hours and this did not allow me to experience the full game yet the content level is astronomical , I see why this game is an indie darling.

it deserve its spot as the uncontested king of rogue like.
Very difficult but very fun gameplay. Banger soundtrack. I love that everything in this game is sort of a glass cannon on high difficulty, including you.
great steam deck game, love the combat + platforming mix

I bought this game during a sale long ago and quickly forgot that it ever existed.

Huge mistake on my part.

With Risk of Rain being one of my favorite games of all time, I should have seen Dead Cells and knew immediately that the same magic that made RoR a trailblazer was also shining just as bright in this one.  

The upside to committing to a masterpiece years after release is that I get all the polish and extra content from day one.  While punishing at times, the combat is fluid, and the various weaponry at your finger tips clearly provides more than enough reason to make this game a perennial favorite for years to come.

UPDATE:  

Now with 35 hours of gameplay, it's evident that the Motion Twins put on a master class for the Rogue-Like genre.  The variety of biomes and unlocks coupled with the unrivaled aesthetics needs to be the gold standard for games that aim to walk in their massive shoes.

Dead Cells sits smack-dab in the middle of my Mount Rushmore of video games.  Here's to an un-ending stream of updates and DLC (and maybe a sequel or two)!!

holy ####ng ♥♥♥♥ this game is an absolute masterpiece after finishing this game on 5 bc I literally hit the absolute cinema gif. This game is sooooooooo good 9/10 one of the best game that I played DEFINITELY should be on your must play list.

I love this game, i really do, but sometimes when i want to play and relax... the game just... doesn't let you relax. This game is an absolute dopamine factory and it is ridiculously addictive, I love it, and probably always will. You don't need the DLC's to have fun in this game either, there is just that much content. Please get the game.

I am not the biggest fan of Roguelikes but play them on occassion. This one was a hard sell for me initially, since it has a high difficulty level for beginners. I didn't want to lower the difficulty in order to git gud and overcome the challenges by sheer will. If you stick with this game for a few hours you will see the appeal. You will reach the second maybe even the third level, you will unlock new weapons and abilities and your skill will slowly increase as well.
Still, it is a frustrating experience always starting from the beginning. After ten to twenty hours you will embrace every new run as a new opportunity to learn and to master the game. After thirty to fourty hous you'll appreciate how well the game is designed. I know few games that have more thought put into its every mechanic.
Graphics are engaging, music is beautiful and eventually you'll learn to overcome your anxienty, stop smashing buttons and master the Dance of Death.
I have only finished two boss cells and I know there is so much more to the game. But eventually it did get a little bit old for me after some time. 80 hours of playtime for less than 20 Euros is absolutely worth it, though.

this game is pain.

With a lot of perseverance and a lot of trial and error you will discover... That you suck at it.

Big recommend.
A classic roguelite in every sense. Such a simple but fun and addicting gameplay loop. Combat feels hefty and impactful, there's plenty to do and find, and the difficulty is high but fair.

This is the third platform I'm playing this on. This game just really scratches an itch as someone who grew up playing metroidvanias. I keep dying, but the progression is rewarding. All the build varieties ensure the gameplay never gets old.
this game aint nothing but straight peak. ALWAYS choose the shield as your secondary :fire:

Ah, I love me some Dead Cells man. The combat in the game is addictive and having so many different weapons and abilities to find each run means you'll never get tired of playing it. A good amount of different areas to explore as well, and i LOVE the Easter eggs from other games included in here. I recommend.
Amazing replayability. While the game is 200% complete, it's sad we didn't get even more DLC or patches.

Très bon jeu, moult équipements à débloquer, des combats vifs et satisfaisant.

Rien à dire, bravo Motion Twin !
Its good and worth the money but it is ridiculously overrated. People acting like its the best thing made in the last ten years. Its a 7/10 game.

Dead Cells has been my favorite game for years. The gameplay flows very intuitively, there's item collecting and it's different every time you play. A must have for any fans of Metroid/Castlevania/roguelikes.
Very addictive, but it can be very easy to rage for every time you die since you lose all progress.
Frustrating in the best way possible, this game is a fairly difficult roguelike that makes me come back each time I die. I liked it a lot, 8/10

Absolutely love. Really good replay value. I started it on iOS when it came out and it’s the kind of game I keep coming back to from time to time. The combat feels great and every run still feels fresh. I also really like the community around it and the DLCs add a lot of extra content that keeps it interesting.
Very hard game, still trying to beat the 4 boss cells difficulty
Game's so good I've bought it like 4 times across consoles without hesitation. If I get a new console, I'd buy it again and start all over without complaint.

It was fun uuntil I noticed how insanly hard it is, but git gud I suppose...

Edit: turned on assist mode against my own ego, slower enemies, did not notice any differnce.
highly recommend if you like hollow knight, it's very similar. mechanics are a little different but they're fun.
To much repetitiveness for a rougelike, every run feels the same and the core gameplay ain't nothing that engaging.
Extremely fun roguelike. Combat is dialed to perfection and I love the references to other games.
almost broke my pc in the first hour of playing got it refunded , bought it again thinking i crashed out
Es tan adictivo como estresante, y soy demasiado manco. Pero me encanta!!, me sirve para estresarme en algo que no sea mi vida

[h1]Do Not Let My Steam Playtime Fool You, This Game Is Digital Crack[/h1]

[b]The Verdict:[/b] An absolute masterpiece of action, speed, and fluid combat. It is the gold standard of action-roguelites, and it will aggressively hold your free time hostage.

[hr][/hr]

[h2]The Warning: A Literal Black Hole for Your Free Time[/h2]

[b]Don't Trust the Steam Clock:[/b] Seriously, do not let my current playtime on this account fool you. I previously had this masterpiece on my Nintendo Switch, and I sunk a completely ungodly, borderline embarrassing amount of hours into it there. It is the ultimate "just one more run" game that easily turns a quick 20-minute session into a 4:00 AM existential crisis.

[b]The Perfect Handheld/PC Translation:[/b] Whether you're playing it on the go or locked into your monitor, the addictive gameplay loop translates perfectly. It’s impossible to put down because the instant you die, your brain immediately starts plotting the next build.

[hr][/hr]

[h2]Why It’s So ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ Good[/h2]

[b]The Smoothest Combat on Earth:[/b] The movement and combat in this game are flawless. It is fast, snappy, and incredibly responsive. Rolling through enemy attacks, parrying with shields, and smashing down from high platforms feels so satisfying that it makes almost every other 2D action game feel sluggish by comparison.

[b]Insane Weapon Variety & Synergies:[/b] From frying pans and giant broadswords to whips that crit from behind and magic spells that freeze entire rooms, the arsenal is massive. The game rewards you for finding ridiculous item synergies (like applying oil with one weapon and igniting it with a turret) that melt bosses in seconds.

[b]Tough, Fair, and Constantly Evolving:[/b] The difficulty curve is brutal but brilliant. Every time you beat the final boss, you unlock a "Boss Stem Cell" that injects new enemy types, removes healing fountains, and jacks up the challenge, completely refreshing the game over and over again.

[hr][/hr]

[b]The Bottom Line:[/b]
[i]Dead Cells[/i] is an essential, flawless blend of Metroidvania exploration and brutal roguelite progression. If you haven't played it yet, buy it immediately—just say goodbye to your loved ones and your sleep schedule first, because it will absolutely consume your life.
